title: Enhanced spatio-temporal electric load forecasts using less data with active deep learning
data_sources: Swiss building electricity consumption (distribution system operators)
core_conclusion: Active deep learning (ADL) enables electric utilities to collect 29–46% less smart-meter data while achieving 26–81% higher load-prediction accuracy compared with conventional passive (random) data collection, and can reduce physical sensor deployment by up to 24% — at the cost of 4–11 times more computation. A new embedding-uncertainty metric derived from a spatio-temporal embedding network guides informative data selection across buildings and time steps.
why_it_matters: Breaks the cost barrier for grid decarbonization by showing that accurate spatio-temporal load forecasts do not require exhaustive metering; introduces strategic active data collection as an alternative to costly sensor scaling; provides open code, a Python package, and Harvard Dataverse data to enable direct reuse; addresses a real inequity in smart-meter adoption across Global South regions.
why_nature: Novel architecture and metric (embedding uncertainty) applied to a high-impact climate problem; rigorous evaluation across all prediction-shift types; explicit theoretical connections between query diversity, catastrophic forgetting, and online regret; reproducibility-first design matching NMI standards.
added_value_to_my_research: Graph-aware ADL on grid topology -> transferable sensor-placement GeoAI operator
Transfer-based ADL across cities -> informative fine-tuning strategy for geospatial foundation models
ADL building-level forecasts aggregated to district net-load maps -> urban spatiotemporal density surfaces
Equity-constrained ADL meter placement -> R2SFCA-grounded efficiency-vs-equity optimization for infrastructure
Embedding-uncertainty surface as spatial uncertainty atlas -> explainability layer for ML-based urban energy policy

related.title: Grid connections and inequitable access to electricity in African cities

related.title: A systematic map of machine learning for urban climate change mitigation
related.relation: systematic landscape of ML for urban climate problems — positions ADL load forecasting within the broader urban-climate ML taxonomy

figures.caption: Global smart meter adoption rates — worldwide, Europe, and Asia maps plus country-level distributions across Africa, Latin America, Asia, and Europe
Spatio-temporal embedding network architecture — modular ANN/CNN/RNN/CRNN branches for time, space, and space-time features
Four ADL query variants in 2D embedded space: random, maximized uncertainty (cluster fringe), minimized uncertainty (cluster centre), and averaged uncertainty
Training and validation losses for spatio-temporal predictions under delta=1 and delta=0 — comparing ADL variants against passive deep learning baseline
Computational complexity reduction experiments (candidate subsampling, cluster reduction, spatial coordinate embedding) and query-sequence importance
